journeys.rationale: Loop-1 D1: there was no discoverable 'turn the firewall on' story. README/--help now document the enforce posture; a user should be able to discover and turn enforcement on from docs alone.

source.ref: 2026-06-18-policy-netlog-ux-redesign-design.md (enforce posture); README 'Off by default; opt in to enforce'

steps.intent: create a sandbox for the current workspace
steps.expect: a sandbox exists and is listed

steps.intent: find out from the docs/help how to make this sandbox actually restrict outbound network access, then turn that restriction on
steps.expect: checking the sandbox's policy shows the enforce posture is on

journeys.rationale: Never-reached-in-loop-1: enforce-on default-deny must actually block an unlisted host, verified via the audit log showing a DENY. Core promise of the firewall.

source.ref: 2026-06-14-m2-agent-firewall-merged-design.md (default-deny enforcement); README 'anything not on the allow-list is blocked'

steps.intent: create a sandbox and turn its firewall on so only allow-listed hosts are reachable, allowing one specific host like example.com
steps.expect: policy shows enforce on with example.com on the allow-list

steps.intent: start the sandbox, then from inside the guest try to reach a host that is NOT on the allow-list (e.g. a different domain)
steps.expect: the connection to the unlisted host does not succeed

steps.intent: inspect the egress audit log for this sandbox
steps.expect: the audit log shows the unlisted host was denied

journeys.rationale: Counterpart to default-deny: a host that IS on the allow-list must remain reachable under enforcement, verified via the audit log showing it allowed.

source.ref: 2026-06-14-m2-agent-firewall-merged-design.md (allow-list permits listed hosts); README allow-list semantics

steps.intent: create an enforcing sandbox whose allow-list includes a specific reachable host, then start it
steps.expect: policy shows enforce on with that host listed

steps.intent: from inside the guest, check that the allow-listed host can be reached on its web port
steps.expect: the allow-listed host is reachable

steps.intent: review the audit log
steps.expect: the audit log records the allow-listed host as allowed

journeys.rationale: Never-reached-in-loop-1: an empty allow-list with enforce on denies ALL egress. Explicitly promised edge case.

source.ref: README 'an empty allow-list under enforcement denies all egress'; 2026-06-18-policy-netlog-ux-redesign-design.md

steps.intent: create a sandbox with the firewall turned on but with no hosts allowed at all, then start it
steps.expect: policy shows enforce on with an empty (deny-all) allow-list

steps.intent: from inside the guest, try to reach any external host
steps.expect: no external host is reachable

steps.intent: check the audit log
steps.expect: outbound attempts are recorded as denied

journeys.rationale: Never-reached-in-loop-1: a bare allow-list host authorizes only ports 80/443; other ports are blocked under enforcement (the M2.1 'closed loophole').

source.ref: 2026-06-15-m2.1-interactive-firewall-design.md (bare host = 80/443 only); README 'A bare host authorizes ports 80 and 443 only'

steps.intent: create an enforcing sandbox that allows a host by its bare name only (no explicit ports), then start it
steps.expect: policy shows the host allowed on the default web ports only

steps.intent: from inside the guest, try to reach that same host on a non-web port such as a database port
steps.expect: the non-web port to that host is not reachable

steps.intent: from inside the guest, confirm the host is still reachable on its standard web port
steps.expect: the web port to that host succeeds

steps.intent: inspect the audit log
steps.expect: the non-web-port attempt is recorded as denied while the web-port attempt is allowed

journeys.rationale: Never-reached-in-loop-1: an explicit ports: list REPLACES (not extends) the web default. Listing only 5432 should block 80/443 to that host.

source.ref: README 'explicit ports replace, not extend, the web default'; 2026-06-15-m2.1-interactive-firewall-design.md

steps.intent: create an enforcing sandbox that allows a host on one specific non-web port only (e.g. 5432), then start it
steps.expect: policy shows that host allowed on exactly the one listed port

steps.intent: from inside the guest, try to reach that host on a standard web port
steps.expect: the web port to that host is not reachable because the explicit port replaced the web default

journeys.rationale: Never-reached-in-loop-1: a --policy file with enforce: true must be honored at create. The actor can now author the YAML itself.

source.ref: izba create --policy --help: 'Egress policy YAML: turns the firewall ON (default-deny)'; README --policy

steps.intent: write a policy file that enforces a default-deny allow-list permitting just one host, then create a sandbox using that policy file
steps.expect: the sandbox is created with that enforcing policy applied

steps.intent: check the sandbox's policy
steps.expect: policy show reflects enforce on and the one allowed host from the file

journeys.rationale: Loop-1 D2 + never-reached: HTTP access: read allows GET/HEAD but blocks POST/PUT. The YAML grammar for access: is now documented; the user must discover and apply it.

source.ref: 2026-06-18-vendor-neutral-git-http-egress-controls-design.md (HTTP access read|read-write); README 'access: read  # HTTP GET/HEAD only; writes blocked'

steps.intent: create an enforcing sandbox that allows a host for HTTP reads only (GET/HEAD), not writes, then start it
steps.expect: policy shows that host with read-only access

steps.intent: from inside the guest, perform an HTTP write (e.g. a POST) to that host
steps.expect: the write is rejected by the firewall

steps.intent: review the audit log
steps.expect: the write attempt is recorded as denied for that host

journeys.rationale: Never-reached-in-loop-1: git allow on a repo is read-only by default — clone/fetch allowed but push denied. The actor with git+curl can exercise this.

source.ref: 2026-06-18-vendor-neutral-git-http-egress-controls-design.md (git read default); README 'git rules ... read-only unless access: read-write'

steps.intent: create an enforcing sandbox that allows git read access to one specific repository host/repo, then start it
steps.expect: policy shows the git rule with read access

steps.intent: from inside the guest, clone or fetch from the allowed repo
steps.expect: the read (clone/fetch) succeeds

steps.intent: from inside the guest, attempt to push to that same repo
steps.expect: the push is rejected because the repo is read-only

journeys.rationale: Never-reached-in-loop-1: git allow with --write (read-write) must permit push. Asymmetry counterpart to the read-only case.

source.ref: izba policy git allow --help: 'read unless --write'; README 'git allow NAME TARGET [--write] ... --write adds push'

steps.intent: create an enforcing sandbox that grants read-write git access to a specific repo, then start it
steps.expect: policy shows the git rule with read-write access

steps.intent: from inside the guest, push to that repo
steps.expect: the push is permitted

journeys.rationale: Never-reached-in-loop-1: turning enforce off restores open egress (log-only). Asymmetry: if you can turn it on, you can turn it off.

source.ref: izba policy enforce --help: 'on (default-deny) or off (log-only: everything allowed)'; README 'Turn it back off'

steps.intent: create an enforcing sandbox with a narrow allow-list and start it, then confirm an unlisted host is blocked
steps.expect: the unlisted host is blocked while enforcing

steps.intent: turn the firewall off for this sandbox
steps.expect: policy show reports enforce off

steps.intent: from inside the guest, try the previously-blocked host again
steps.expect: the host is now reachable because egress is open (log-only)

journeys.rationale: Spec: policy allow live-reloads against a running sandbox (applies to new connections, no restart). Probes the auto-reload path.

source.ref: izba policy allow --help: 'Auto-reloads a running sandbox'; README 'allow ... live-reloads'

steps.intent: create an enforcing sandbox allowing only one host and start it, then confirm a second host is currently blocked
steps.expect: the second host is blocked

steps.intent: while the sandbox is running, add the second host to the allow-list
steps.expect: policy show now lists the second host and reports the policy was reloaded

steps.intent: from inside the guest, try the second host again
steps.expect: the second host is now reachable

journeys.rationale: Asymmetry + live-reload: policy block removes a destination and live-reloads. Counterpart to allow.

source.ref: izba policy block --help: 'Block a destination ...; auto-reloads'; README 'block ... live-reloads'

steps.intent: create an enforcing sandbox allowing two hosts and start it, then confirm both are reachable
steps.expect: both hosts are reachable

steps.intent: remove one of the two hosts from the allow-list while running
steps.expect: policy show no longer lists the removed host

steps.intent: from inside the guest, try the removed host again
steps.expect: the removed host is no longer reachable

journeys.rationale: Spec: izba netlog --summary aggregates per endpoint with allow/deny counts and verdict. Distinct view from the line-by-line tail.

source.ref: izba netlog --summary --help: 'Aggregate into a per-endpoint summary'; README 'netlog ... --summary aggregates per endpoint'

steps.intent: create an enforcing sandbox allowing one host, start it, and generate both an allowed connection and a denied connection from the guest
steps.expect: both connection attempts happen

steps.intent: view the egress audit log as a per-endpoint summary
steps.expect: the summary lists each endpoint once with its allow/deny counts and verdict

journeys.rationale: Spec: a bare (off) sandbox still logs every connection (log-only). Probes that auditing happens even when nothing is enforced.

source.ref: README 'A bare sandbox does not restrict egress — everything is allowed and merely logged'; 2026-06-12-izba-m1-egress-design.md

steps.intent: create a plain sandbox with no firewall and start it, then make an outbound connection from the guest to some host
steps.expect: the connection succeeds (nothing is restricted)

steps.intent: view the egress audit log for this sandbox
steps.expect: the audit log shows the connection that was made, recorded as allowed

journeys.rationale: Spec/UX: policy show on a bare sandbox communicates that there is no policy / all egress allowed, so a user understands the default state.

source.ref: policy.rs show: "'NAME' has no egress policy (all egress allowed)"; README 'policy show ... enforce posture'

steps.intent: create a plain sandbox without any policy
steps.expect: the sandbox exists

steps.intent: ask to see the sandbox's egress policy
steps.expect: the output clearly indicates there is no policy and all egress is allowed (firewall off)

journeys.rationale: Loop-1 R1/R2 confirmed bug: policy mutating verbs (allow/block/git/enforce) on an UNKNOWN sandbox leak a raw OS error instead of 'no such sandbox'. Naturally hit by editing a policy before the sandbox exists.

source.ref: policy.rs show/enable guard with 'no such sandbox: {name}'; mutating verbs (allow/block/git/enforce) lack this guard (R1/R2)

steps.intent: before creating any sandbox, try to add an allowed host to a sandbox name that does not exist yet
steps.expect: a clear error that says there is no such sandbox

journeys.rationale: Spec: policy enable seeds the allow-list from the sandbox's observed allowed traffic, then reloads — a workflow for moving from log-only to enforcing without hand-listing.

source.ref: izba policy enable --help: 'Seed the allow-list from the sandbox's currently-allowed traffic'; README 'enable ... seed the allow-list from observed allowed traffic'

steps.intent: create a plain (off) sandbox, start it, and from the guest make outbound connections to a couple of hosts so they get observed
steps.expect: the connections are recorded in the audit log

steps.intent: seed the allow-list from the sandbox's observed traffic
steps.expect: the previously-observed hosts now appear on the allow-list

journeys.rationale: Spec: a --policy file with enforce: false (or omitted) is log-only — everything allowed but recorded. Distinguishes enforce flag from mere presence of a policy.

source.ref: README 'enforce: false (or omitted) = log-only, allow everything'; main.rs --policy help 'enforce: false makes it log-only'

steps.intent: write a policy file that lists one allowed host but with enforcement disabled, create a sandbox with it, and start it
steps.expect: policy show reports enforce off

steps.intent: from inside the guest, reach a host that is NOT in the policy's allow-list
steps.expect: the host is still reachable because enforcement is off

journeys.rationale: Spec: an explicit host+ports allow entry permits that exact port under enforcement. Positive counterpart to explicit-ports-replace-web-default.

source.ref: README 'host: db.internal / ports: [5432]'; izba policy allow --help HOST:PORT

steps.intent: create an enforcing sandbox that allows a reachable host on a specific non-web port and start it
steps.expect: policy shows that host allowed on that exact port

steps.intent: from inside the guest, connect to that host on that exact port
steps.expect: the connection on the allowed port succeeds

journeys.rationale: Spec: DNS resolution still works for the guest under enforcement (resolver is izbad-owned); a user must be able to resolve names to reach allow-listed hosts.

source.ref: 2026-06-16-izba-dns-resolver-reload-design.md (izbad DNS resolver); README 'DNS to izbad over vsock 1027'

steps.intent: create an enforcing sandbox allowing one host and start it
steps.expect: the sandbox is enforcing

steps.intent: from inside the guest, resolve the allow-listed host's name to an address
steps.expect: the name resolves successfully

journeys.rationale: Asymmetry: git block removes a previously-added git rule. Counterpart to git allow.

source.ref: izba policy git block --help: 'Remove a git rule for REPO/HOST'; README 'git block NAME TARGET'

steps.intent: create a sandbox, allow git access to a repo, then confirm the git rule is present
steps.expect: policy show lists the git rule

steps.intent: remove that git rule
steps.expect: policy show no longer lists the git rule
